name: stat-card
role: KPI tile primitive. The companion to data-table in dashboard surfaces — a row of 3-6 stat-cards typically sits above the table to surface the high-level numbers (total monthly spend, active subscriptions, average tenure, etc.). Composes label + value + optional delta + optional sparkline + optional context caption. Three variants — bordered (default; border, no fill), flat (no border, no fill — used when stacked tightly), elevated (filled surface with shadow). Three sizes — sm (in a tight row of 5-6), md (default), lg (hero metric). Four states — default / loading (skeleton) / error / no-data.

meta.interactivity: When interactive='true', the entire card becomes a focusable <button> or <a> that navigates to the metric's detail view. Hover applies shadow; focus-visible applies outline.
meta.compositionDecision_validationStatusIcon: stat-card does NOT compose pattern-form-validation's validation-status-icon for delta indicators despite some semantic overlap (success/critical tones). The icon vocabularies are different — validation icons are check/x/warning/spinner; delta indicators are up-arrow/down-arrow/flat. Visual literacy is clearer when these vocabularies stay separate. stat-card ships its own delta-indicator inside stat-delta.

ariaPatterns.interactiveCard: Interactive cards use <a href> or <button type='button'> as the outer element OR carry tabindex='0' + role='link' / 'button' with explicit handlers. Whole-card clickability requires the inner content's links to NOT be tab stops (use aria-hidden on duplicate links).
ariaPatterns.loadingAriaLive: When state='loading' transitions to state='default' with fresh data, the value should be announced via aria-live='polite' on the stat-value element.
ariaPatterns.noDataMessage: no-data state renders the value as '—' (em dash) and adds an aria-label naming the absence ('Monthly recurring: no data available').

name: stat-label
role: Small uppercase caption that names the metric. Always sits above the value. Uppercase + letter-spacing-wide treatment. Optional info-tip trigger to the right for a tooltip explaining the metric's definition.

ariaPatterns.tooltipTrigger: If show-tooltip=true, an info button (?) sits next to the label with aria-label naming the metric ('What is <metric-name>?') and aria-haspopup='dialog' or 'tooltip'.

name: stat-value
role: The large numeric value at the centre of the card. Uses tabular-nums for column-aligned digits when multiple stat-cards stack. Sizes via parent's data-size. Optionally accompanied by a unit-prefix or unit-suffix (currency symbol, percent sign, 'GB', etc.). The unit pieces are visually de-emphasised but accessible.

meta.unitHandling: Visual unit symbols ($ / % / GB / /mo) are aria-hidden because they're glyph-decoration. A visually-hidden span announces the full value with units spelled out for AT.
meta.tabularNums: font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums ensures digits align column-wise when multiple stat-cards stack — critical for visual scanning across a row of KPIs.

name: stat-delta
role: Change indicator below the value. Shows direction (up / down / flat) + magnitude + optional period reference. Tone-coloured — positive (success green up-arrow), negative (critical red down-arrow), neutral (text-recede flat dash). The semantic of positive vs negative is INVERSE for some metrics — e.g., monthly recurring spend going UP is bad — so the tone attribute is explicit, not auto-derived from direction.

semanticStructure: <!-- up / down / flat arrow per direction attribute -->
meta.toneVsDirectionPolicy: tone (positive / negative / neutral) is EXPLICIT, not derived from direction. For most metrics 'up = positive', but for spend / cost / latency, 'up = negative'. The host sets tone based on the metric's semantic; this primitive only renders the visual treatment.

ariaPatterns.deltaAccessibleName: The element should have an accessible name that includes the direction in plain language. Use sr-only spans: 'up' or 'down' alongside the arrow svg. Or aria-label on the container: aria-label='+$24 this month, increased'.
ariaPatterns.arrowAriaHidden: Arrow SVGs are aria-hidden='true' — magnitude text + period text carry the meaning.

name: stat-trendline
role: Inline sparkline visualizing the metric's trend over a short period (last 7 / 14 / 30 days). Renders as an inline SVG <path> with no axes, no labels. Tone-coloured to match the stat-delta's tone or neutral.

semanticStructure: <desc id='<id>-desc>Monthly recurring spend trended from $286 to $310, generally rising.</desc>
meta.dataDensity: Sparklines should show at least 7 data points (one per day for a week) and at most ~30 (one per day for a month). Fewer than 7 isn't a trend; more than 30 is noise.

ariaPatterns.roleImg: <svg role='img'> + aria-labelledby pointing to inline <title> + <desc> elements gives AT a textual description of what the sparkline shows.
ariaPatterns.noOnHover: Hover tooltips on individual data points are out of scope for this primitive (use a full chart pattern in P1).

name: stat-context
role: Small caption below the value or delta. Used for period notes ('Last 30 days'), data source ('From bank feeds'), or update timestamps ('Updated 2 min ago'). One line max.
